desc: This dataset contains sales transaction data from Blinkit, an online grocery delivery platform. It contains sales data from Blinkit, including product details, order quantities, revenue, and timestamps.
goal: Examine customer purchase frequency, basket composition, and product co-occurrence patterns to understand buying behavior and preferences. By identifying which products are frequently bought together, this descriptive analysis helps uncover common customer segments and preferences, aiding in product mix optimization and marketing strategy development.
name: Visual Analytics System blinkit_goal2_1742636333

plan.path: The user first examines purchasing trends and customer segmentation in the Overview (View 1), selects a time window to analyze category co-occurrence patterns in the Exploration heatmap (View 2), then drills down to specific product combinations in the Detail view (View 4). Simultaneously, payment method preferences are explored through stacked bars (View 3), leading to outlier detection in customer spending patterns at the Insight level (View 5). Selection in any view filters subsequent visualizations through parameter propagation.

effect.description: Selects time range and propagates to subsequent views

visualization.description: Dual-axis visualization combining order frequency timeline (line chart) with customer order count distribution (adjacent histogram)
encodings: x: Temporal(order_date, yearmonthdate) showing purchase trends

effect.description: Filters subsequent views to selected category pairs

visualization.description: Heatmap matrix showing category purchase co-occurrence patterns

effect.description: Highlights corresponding transactions in linked views

visualization.description: Small multiples of stacked bars showing payment-category relationships
Top 20 SKU combinations table for selected categories
Scatterplot matrix of purchase behavior dimensions with trend lines

visualization.reason: Original horizontal facet grouping creates visual fragmentation. Vertical grouping maintains payment method comparison while consolidating category distributions. Avoids small multiples readability issues with 11 potential categories
visualization.requirement: Vertical grouped bar charts with payment method x-axis and customer segment color encoding, aggregated by product category percentages

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW sales_overview AS SELECT order_date, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS transaction_count FROM blinkit_orders;
sqls.name: sales_overview

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW customer_order_counts AS SELECT customer_id, COUNT(order_id) AS total_orders FROM blinkit_orders GROUP BY customer_id;
sqls.name: customer_order_counts

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW category_co_occurrence AS SELECT category1, category2, COUNT(*) AS co_occurrence FROM (SELECT category AS category1 FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id WHERE category IN (SELECT category FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id GROUP BY category) GROUP BY category, order_id UNION SELECT category AS category2 FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id WHERE category IN (SELECT category FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id GROUP BY category) GROUP BY category, order_id) GROUP BY category1, category2;
sqls.name: category_co_occurrence

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW payment_category_relationships AS SELECT payment_method, category, SUM(order_total) AS total_order_value, COUNT(customer_id) AS num_customers FROM blinkit_orders JOIN blinkit_order_items ON blinkit_orders.order_id = blinkit_order_items.order_id JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id GROUP BY payment_method, category;
sqls.name: payment_category_relationships

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW top_sku_combinations AS SELECT product_id, product_name, SUM(quantity) AS frequency FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id WHERE category IN (SELECT category FROM blinkit_order_items JOIN blinkit_products ON blinkit_order_items.product_id = blinkit_products.product_id GROUP BY category HAVING COUNT(*) > 1) GROUP BY product_id, product_name ORDER BY frequency DESC LIMIT 20;
sqls.name: top_sku_combinations

sqls.SQL: CREATE VIEW customer_purchasing_behavior AS SELECT total_orders, avg_order_value, order_total FROM blinkit_customers;
sqls.name: customer_purchasing_behavior

tasks.method: Time series line chart showing purchase frequency trends. Dual-axis visualization overlaying histogram of order count distribution per customer (x: total_orders bins, y: count of customers). Encoding: Line color for temporal patterns, bar opacity for density representation
tasks.objective: Establish baseline purchase behavior patterns and identify high-frequency purchasers

tasks.method: Matrix heatmap of product category co-occurrence in orders (rows: purchased categories, columns: co-purchased categories). Linked detail view on click showing exact SKU combinations using adjacency list with frequency counts (implementation of I3). Color intensity encodes Jaccard similarity coefficient
tasks.objective: Identify complementary product categories and segment-specific purchase patterns

tasks.method: Small multiples of horizontal stacked bars (implementation of V3) split by customer segment. X-axis: category composition percentage, Y-axis: payment methods. Encoding: Color hue for product categories, bar length for proportional representation
tasks.objective: Reveal payment method preferences and category associations across different value segments

tasks.method: Scatter plot matrix with brush-based filtering. Axes: Order frequency vs. avg order value vs. max single order value. Encoding: Color by customer_segment, size by total_spent. Trend lines show expected value distributions
tasks.objective: Detect outlier customers with unusual purchase patterns for targeted analysis

tasks.method: Radar chart showing profitability vs. popularity metrics. Radial axes: Margin%, InventoryTurnover(derived), CategoryFrequency(derived). Encoding: Line thickness for customer segment density, angular position for product categories
tasks.objective: Optimize product mix by visualizing trade-offs between margins and purchase frequency

tasks.method: Bubble chart with perishability quadrant analysis. X: ShelfLife, Y: StockRotationRate(derived). Encoding: Bubble size=damage rate, color=intensity=recommended_order_quantity(derived)
tasks.objective: Identify optimal inventory levels based on product shelf life and spoilage patterns
